title: Two-Way Prepositions — Movement vs Location
description: Nine prepositions can take EITHER the Accusative or the Dative. The choice is logical: movement toward a place uses Accusative; resting at a place uses Dative. Master the question 'wohin?' vs 'wo?'.

lessons.title: wohin? (Akkusative) vs wo? (Dative)
lessons.subtitle: The same preposition, two cases — decided by movement.
lessons.hook: 'I go INTO the house' vs 'I am IN the house.' Same little word 'in' — but German changes the case to show the difference. Movement into a place → Accusative. Being somewhere → Dative. One logical rule replaces a whole page of memorization.
lessons.explain: Nine 'two-way' prepositions take Accusative OR Dative: in, an, auf, über, unter, vor, hinter, neben, zwischen. The rule: if there is MOVEMENT toward a destination (answers 'wohin?' — where to?), use the Accusative. If it describes a fixed LOCATION (answers 'wo?' — where?), use the Dative. Compare: 'Ich gehe in die Küche' (into the kitchen — Akk, movement) vs 'Ich bin in der Küche' (in the kitchen — Dat, location). Verbs of motion (gehen, fahren, legen, stellen) pair with Accusative; static verbs (sein, liegen, stehen, bleiben) pair with Dative.

examples.german: Das Buch liegt auf dem Tisch.
examples.english: The book is (lying) on the table. (wo?)

vocabulary.exampleSentence: Ich gehe in den Park. / Ich bin im Park.
vocabulary.exampleTranslation: I go into the park. / I'm in the park.
vocabulary.memoryHook: Movement → Akk (in den Park); location → Dat (im Park = in dem Park).

vocabulary.exampleSentence: Das Glas steht auf dem Tisch.
vocabulary.exampleTranslation: The glass is on the table.
vocabulary.memoryHook: auf = on a horizontal surface. Location → Dative (auf dem Tisch).

vocabulary.exampleSentence: Das Bild hängt an der Wand.
vocabulary.exampleTranslation: The picture hangs on the wall.
vocabulary.memoryHook: an = at/against a vertical surface (an der Wand).
Two-way: neben + Dat for location (neben mir).
-e ending → die. In Dative: in der Küche.

exercises.prompt: Which question does the ACCUSATIVE answer with a two-way preposition?
exercises.explanation: Accusative = movement toward a destination ('wohin?'). Dative = fixed location ('wo?'). 'Ich gehe in die Küche' (wohin → Akk).

exercises.prompt: Location: 'Ich bin in ___ Küche.' (I'm in the kitchen.) — die Küche, Dative
exercises.explanation: 'sein' is static → Dative. Feminine Dative: die → der. 'Ich bin in der Küche.'

exercises.prompt: 'Das Buch liegt auf ___ Tisch.' (The book is on the table.) — der Tisch
exercises.explanation: liegen describes location → Dative. Masculine Dative: der → dem. 'auf dem Tisch.'

lessons.title: Contractions & Getting Around Town
lessons.subtitle: im, ins, am, ans, zum, zur — and describing where things are.
lessons.hook: Germans love shortcuts. 'in dem' becomes 'im', 'in das' becomes 'ins', 'an dem' becomes 'am'. These contractions are not slang — they're the standard, correct way to speak. Learn the handful and you'll instantly sound more natural.
lessons.explain: Common preposition + article contractions: in dem → im, in das → ins, an dem → am, an das → ans, zu dem → zum, zu der → zur, bei dem → beim. Use them by default in speech: 'Ich gehe ins Kino' (into the cinema — movement/Akk), 'Ich bin im Kino' (in the cinema — location/Dat). Time expressions also use them: 'am Montag' (on Monday), 'im Sommer' (in summer). These are everywhere when you talk about places and schedules.

examples.german: Am Montag gehe ich zur Arbeit.
examples.english: On Monday I go to work.

vocabulary.memoryHook: in das → ins. Movement → Accusative (neuter das).
in dem → im. Location or time (im Winter, im Kino).

vocabulary.exampleTranslation: On the weekend I sleep in.
vocabulary.memoryHook: an dem → am. Days & weekend: am Montag, am Wochenende.
zu dem → zum (masc/neut). Feminine: zu der → zur.
das Kino → ins Kino (to), im Kino (at).

exercises.explanation: in + das → ins (used for movement into a neuter place): 'Ich gehe ins Kino.' Compare in + dem → im.

exercises.prompt: Which is correct for 'We're at the restaurant' (location)?
exercises.explanation: Location + 'sein' → Dative. in dem → im: 'Wir sind im Restaurant.' 'ins' would wrongly signal movement.

exercises.prompt: Complete: '___ Montag habe ich frei.' (On Monday I'm off.) — an + dem
exercises.explanation: Days of the week use 'an dem' → 'am': 'Am Montag habe ich frei.'
